title: Cephalosorus carpesioides
intro: Cephalosorus carpesioides, also known as the Carpesioid Spleenwort, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Cystopteridaceae. Traditionally, this plant is used in folk medicine for its health benefits, which include treating fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Cephalosorus carpesioides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include infusions and decoctions, often used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
taxonomy: Cephalosorus carpesioides is a genus of extinct plants in the family Cephalosoraceae. Cephalosorus carpesioides belongs to the division Pteridophyta, which comprises vascular plants with fern-like characteristics. The classification of Cephalosorus carpesioides is further divided into the class Psilopsida, a group of ancient vascular plants. The class Psilopsida is a part of the division Pteridophyta and is characterized by the absence of true leaves and roots. Cephalosorus carpesioides is often found in fossil records from the Devonian period.
